---
title: 'The Debit Card | Your best friend, or mortal enemy... preventing overdrafts.'
date: '2016-05-08T06:40:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6279099291511677043'
blogger_filename: '/2016/05/the-debit-card-your-best-friend-or_7.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Banking'
---

<br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/neil-turner-debit-card-24efb9ab70f3.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" height="300" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/neil-turner-debit-card-8f4b8d05c8de.jpg" width="400" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://www.flickr.com/photos/neilt/7123758/in/photolist-CvDq-9DvemK-92e5t9-52bYaY-nwu7fJ-anmqXB-ntF8mG-9htwN2-cDxRAy-n5ofth-5UGoNJ-9fFFfy-64Fobe-96Z56F-79eRgv-neVHGf-aMJeQK-inZMwE-gzTXsh-7kbZTa-6o1rU1-92aXrt-fAQkjA-dttiGb-4bPyGE-o2jgZV-nffABV-83B2U-oiKsrq-ncBYc4-92e5vL-9VAsVf-rvGCP8-ahsPd1-bZhKMy-nwrLxy-a9Ygrh-boJecw-frp8BB-gPXgyh-ogKQT5-efvXcu-dT9uS4-cJ7fPJ-ntQ1N8-neVMaf-9z4BAC-mMfT1o-neVz1B-9VxAFa" target="_blank">Neil Turner Debit Card</a></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><br /><h3>The use of debit cards can also be a major source of overdrafts.&nbsp;</h3><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">If you go to the store and the machine asks you â€œDebitâ€ or â€œCreditâ€ you can use either one as long as your bank card has a Visa/MasterCard logo.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"></div><ul><li>If it does not have that logo you may only use â€œDebitâ€. &nbsp;If you select â€œDebitâ€ the machine will then ask for your pin number.&nbsp;</li></ul><ul><li>If you select the â€œcreditâ€ option you will not need the pin. You only sign for the purchase or enter your zip code.</li></ul><br /><div class="MsoNormal"><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><h4>~Debit~</h4><div class="MsoNormal"><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">If you say â€œDebitâ€ and use your PIN (Personal Identification Number) the machine will run that purchase request through a Point of Sale Network, (P.O.S.)<br /><br />The most common POS networks are STAR, Interlink, and Plus. These are the same Networks used to get money from an ATM machine. When you use the card as a POS transaction it will be sent to the bank the same business day in which it is done.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">This means it will show immediately as pending then post that business day. If done before the cut off time on Friday the transaction will be processed Friday night and post Saturday morning. If done on Friday after the cut off time it will go through possessing Monday night and post Tuesday morning.<o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><h4>~Credit~</h4><div class="MsoNormal"><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">If you say credit at that same machine and swipe that same debit card with the Visa/MasterCard logo, you will think you did the same thing.<br /><br />This time it asks for your signature and or zip code. This time the transaction will go through the Visa or MasterCard Network.<br /><br />This is different in a few ways.<o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal">Credit is similar in that the transaction will still â€œPendâ€ to your account<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/Wolfe%20Family%20Conceptions%20Projects/!!Writing/Banking%20Basics/Website/Banking%20Basics%20for%20Believers%20stripped.doc#_ftn1" name="_ftnref1" title=""><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">[1]</span><!--[endif]--></a>. The credit function of your card is also similar in that both types of transactions come directly from your account.<o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>Important note: This is NOT a credit / charge card. When you use a charge card or a credit card you build a balance which you pay back later. And a debit card whether you use â€œDebitâ€ or â€œCreditâ€ still comes out of your Checking Account. </i></blockquote><div class="MsoNormal">But then the rest leads to some confusion...<br /><br /></div><h4>~Confusion~</h4><div class="MsoNormal"><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">When you use the credit key the merchant receives an authorization to use the card.<br /><br />Whatever the original amount authorized is, will often show as pending to the account. At a grocery store the amount will usually pend correct because the amount authorized was your final amount unless you realize after the card was swiped that there was a problem with the amount.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">However if the amount was wrong (or you authorized it for an amount other than the final amount) than the amount showing as pending will be wrong, it will be the first amount swiped.<o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">Restaurants and Gas stations often pend for wrong amounts, such as for $1 or 20% over your bill. Online or Catalog orders will often show as pending the day of the order, fall off, and then post once the item ships, which can be months later if it is back ordered.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">Some smaller merchants donâ€™t batch daily or get backed up and may not catch a missed receipt for some time. Merchant or machine errors may cause items to pend two or three at a time, usually this corrects itself within a few days. Rental car companies will authorize a deposit which will pend and disappear.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">These are just a few of the more common reasons your card transactions can be just as hard to track as checks.<o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><h4>~Other Issues~</h4><div class="MsoNormal"><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal">These are some examples of the many reasons pending amounts will be different than the final purchase.<br /><br />Also sometimes a problem with a purchase will cause more than one of the same amount from the same store to pend. These authorizations will typically show as pending for 3-5 business days before posting for the correct amount.<br /><br />Sometimes when more than one of the same transactions pend, one will post and the others will still pend then disappear a few days later. This is because the merchant must submit an intention to finalize before the item can post.&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">If the card was swiped and you then canceled the transaction or voided it than it will still show as pending to your account for 3-5 business days unless that merchant calls and releases the transaction. If the item is right but the merchant is slow to submit the intention to finalize the transaction may show as pending in your balance, disappear after 3-5 business days, and then be added back to your balance and post 2 weeks, 2 months, or a year later. This is why you keep every receipt and write every transaction in your register.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><h4>You cannot know what hasn't posted without a written record.</h4><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal">These are problems only when you are calling to check balances and assuming that the balance you hear is your correct balance.<br /><br />This is not always true.<br /><br />People who write checks are used to understanding that checks may be outstanding and if they call for the balance they have to account for checks that have been written and not cleared.</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal"><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal">People who are younger and donâ€™t write checks arenâ€™t so used to the concept, but they should be. The balance in your register is the only balance you should ever trust.<br /><br />This may come as a surprise to some:<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><b>When you call or go online or check the balance at the ATM, you are not getting your real balance.&nbsp;</b></i></blockquote><br />That balance doesnâ€™t mean anything to you unless you understand how it relates to the transactions that have and have not yet cleared.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">The reason you check with the bank is not to check a balance but to check and see which items have cleared and posted and which items have not.&nbsp;</div><div class="MsoNormal"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal">This allows you to update your <a href="http://bankingbasicsforbelievers.blogspot.com/2016/05/register-balance-that-book.html" target="_blank">register</a>.<o:p></o:p></div><br /><div><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><br clear="all" /><hr align="left" size="1" width="33%" /><!--[endif]--> <br /><div id="ftn1"><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-left: 14.15pt; text-indent: -14.15pt;"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/Wolfe%20Family%20Conceptions%20Projects/!!Writing/Banking%20Basics/Website/Banking%20Basics%20for%20Believers%20stripped.doc#_ftnref1" name="_ftn1" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="color: black; font-family: &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">[1]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><span style="font-size: 10.0pt;"> Reflected in available balance but not yet processed.</span><o:p></o:p></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-left: 14.15pt; text-indent: -14.15pt;"><span style="font-size: 10.0pt;"><br /></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-left: 14.15pt; text-indent: -14.15pt;"><span style="font-size: 10.0pt;"></span><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="-webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; color: black; font-family: 'Times New Roman'; font-size: medium; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; font-weight: normal; letter-spacing: normal; line-height: normal; orphans: auto; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; white-space: normal; widows: 1; word-spacing: 0px;"></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="-webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; color: black; font-family: 'Times New Roman'; font-size: medium; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; font-weight: normal; letter-spacing: normal; line-height: normal; orphans: auto; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; white-space: normal; widows: 1; word-spacing: 0px;"><div style="margin: 0px;"><b><br /></b></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><b>The Insider</b></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><br /></div><h4>The Insider is a BIG fan of Dave Ramsey:</h4><ul><li>Go read/listen/watch Dave Ramsey and he'll teach you how to do it right!</li></ul><ul><li>Listen to The Dave Ramsey Show (<a href="http://www.daveramsey.com/show/radio/" target="_blank">HERE</a>)</li></ul><ul><li>Or by his most popular book:&nbsp;<a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/1595555277/ref=as_li_tl?ie=UTF8&amp;camp=1789&amp;creative=390957&amp;creativeASIN=1595555277&amp;linkCode=as2&amp;tag=your0f4d-20&amp;linkId=EH57Y7XH3LRAVQMF" rel="" target="_blank">(HERE) The Total Money Makeover: Classic Edition: A Proven Plan for Financial Fitness</a><img alt="" border="0" src="http://ir-na.amazon-adsystem.com/e/ir?t=your0f4d-20&amp;l=as2&amp;o=1&amp;a=1595555277" height="1" style="border: none; cursor: move; margin: 0px;" width="1" /></li></ul></div></div></div></div>
